Company.definition: A legally distinct commercial organization referenced in the filing, including the reporting company itself and named external companies such as partners, distributors, resellers, competitors, subsidiaries, or marketplace operators when they act as companies.

BusinessSegment.definition: A formally named internal business segment, division, reporting segment, or line of business used by the company to organize operations or external reporting.

Offering.definition: A specific named product, service, platform, application, subscription, brand, or solution that the company provides commercially.

CustomerType.definition: A standardized category representing the kind of customer or user targeted by the company or an offering.

Channel.definition: A standardized category representing how a company or offering reaches customers commercially.

Place.definition: A named geographic place or market area in which the company or a business segment operates.

RevenueModel.definition: A standardized category representing how a company, business segment, or offering earns revenue.

CustomerType.consumers: Individual end users buying or using a product or service for personal, household, or non-business use.
CustomerType.small businesses: Commercial organizations clearly described as small business, SMB, or an equivalent small-scale business segment.
CustomerType.mid-market companies: Commercial organizations explicitly described as mid-market, midsize, or an equivalent segment between SMB and enterprise scale.
CustomerType.large enterprises: Large organizations with enterprise-scale procurement, operations, compliance, or IT needs.
CustomerType.developers: Software builders who create, test, extend, integrate, or deploy applications, code, or technical solutions.
CustomerType.IT professionals: Technical staff primarily responsible for operating, administering, securing, supporting, or governing information systems and infrastructure.
CustomerType.government agencies: Public-sector bodies with governmental authority or administrative responsibility at federal, state, regional, local, or supranational level.
CustomerType.educational institutions: Organizations whose primary function is formal instruction, training, or education, including schools, colleges, and universities.
CustomerType.healthcare organizations: Organizations whose primary function is delivering, administering, financing, or coordinating health care services.
CustomerType.financial services firms: Organizations primarily engaged in financial transactions, intermediation, insurance, investment, payments, lending, brokerage, or related financial services.
CustomerType.manufacturers: Businesses primarily engaged in transforming materials, components, or substances into physical products.
CustomerType.retailers: Businesses primarily engaged in selling goods in relatively small quantities to end customers through physical or digital retail operations.

Channel.direct sales: Sales where the company sells directly to the end customer through its own sales force, account teams, or direct contracting process.
Channel.online: Sales transacted through the company's own website, app, digital storefront, or self-service online purchasing experience.
Channel.retail: Sales through physical retail stores or store-like consumer-facing locations that sell directly to end customers.
Channel.distributors: Intermediaries that purchase, hold, allocate, or supply products for onward sale through other partners rather than primarily selling to end customers themselves.
Channel.resellers: Third parties that resell the company's products or services to end customers without being the original manufacturer or the primary long-term operator of the solution.
Channel.OEMs: Original equipment manufacturers that embed, bundle, preinstall, or incorporate the company's offering into their own hardware or integrated systems before sale.
Channel.system integrators: Partners whose primary role is combining, customizing, implementing, or integrating multiple technologies into a customer-specific solution.
Channel.managed service providers: Third parties that operate, administer, monitor, maintain, or support technology solutions for customers on an ongoing service basis.
Channel.marketplaces: Third-party digital platforms through which customers discover, procure, subscribe to, or transact for products or services.

RevenueModel.subscription: Revenue earned from recurring payments for ongoing access to a product or service over time.
RevenueModel.advertising: Revenue earned by selling advertising inventory, sponsored visibility, promotional placement, or audience access to third-party advertisers.
RevenueModel.licensing: Revenue earned by granting another party the right to use software, technology, content, trademarks, patents, or other protected assets without transferring ownership.
RevenueModel.consumption-based: Revenue earned according to measured customer usage, volume, metering, or pay-as-you-go consumption rather than a fixed recurring amount alone.
RevenueModel.hardware sales: Revenue earned from selling physical devices, equipment, components, appliances, or other tangible hardware products.
RevenueModel.service fees: Revenue earned from performing services for customers, such as implementation, consulting, support, maintenance, training, professional services, or managed services.
RevenueModel.royalties: Revenue earned as recurring or usage-linked payments from another party's authorized use of the company's intellectual property, content, brand, or technology.
RevenueModel.transaction fees: Revenue earned by charging a fee each time a payment, trade, booking, exchange, or other platform-mediated transaction is processed or facilitated.

HAS_SEGMENT.definition: Links a company to a formally named internal business segment that is part of the company's organizational or reporting structure.

OFFERS.definition: Links a company or business segment to a specific offering that it provides commercially.

PART_OF.definition: Links an offering to the business segment that contains, manages, or reports it as part of that segment's portfolio or organizational scope.

SERVES.definition: Links a company or offering to a canonical customer category that it targets, supports, or serves commercially.

OPERATES_IN.definition: Links a company or business segment to a named geographic place in which it operates, conducts business, or has meaningful market presence.

SELLS_THROUGH.definition: Links a company or offering to a canonical sales or distribution channel through which it reaches customers.

PARTNERS_WITH.definition: Links a company to another company with which it has a named strategic, commercial, distribution, technology, or go-to-market partnership.

MONETIZES_VIA.definition: Links a company, business segment, or offering to the canonical revenue model through which it earns money.

global_rules: Closed ontology: extract only the listed node types and relations.
Closed labels: CustomerType, Channel, and RevenueModel must use only the approved canonical labels.
If a customer, channel, or monetization pattern cannot be mapped clearly to one canonical label, omit it.
Omit ambiguous facts. Precision and standardization are more important than recall.
